title: compat,build: handle `docker's` preconfigured `cacheTo`,`cacheFrom`
body:
Docker's newer clients popuates `cacheFrom` and `cacheTo` parameter by default as empty array for all commands but buildah's design of distributed cache expects this to be a repo not image hence parse only the first populated repo and igore if empty array.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
[NO TESTS NEEDED]
All the remote-bud tests must pass for cacheTo and cacheFrom, docker-client is not present in CI hence cannot be tested.


```release-note
compat,build: make compat build api functional
```

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15928

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Thanks @flouthoc !

Note: this affects the podman release 4.3.0: building anything using the docker client and targetting podman as a server is broken, even when not using any cache-to/cache-from flag.

So I know you usually don't backport many fixes in release branches, but here not being able to use docker as a client to build images might be a big breakage that you may want to also backport in branch 4.3.
/hold Lets hold this one and let me try to extend buildah's API to support multiple cache sources.

> > All the remote-bud tests must pass for cacheTo and cacheFrom, docker-client is not present in CI hence cannot be tested.
> 
> This is not true, we have api tests under `test/apiv2`, you can use them to test any input.

Since newer version of docker-client now sets empty cacheFrom=`[]` for every option secretly so I guess we can add a test for this at API end and get this in so its easier to backport this PR. ( I will extend buildah's --cache-to and --cache-from to accept multiple repos in different PR )

Added a test and also verified against `docker-client` manually. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

Note: This workaround will be removed as soon as buildah's distributed cache starts accepting multiple-repos. Intention of this PR is to patch broken `docker-client` support and ensure easier backport.
@flouthoc Are there plans to change Buildah in the not too distant future?  If so, I'd rather change that then this.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Yes I am extending buildah's API but this flag was non-functional before and but now it broke the build API for `docker` client. Following patch just makes it `non-functional` again for `docker-client` users and makes `docker` client functional again. I think this PR will be easier to backport and unblock end-users quickly. But we can wait for new buildah API for the right fix.
From what I see, podman 4.3 has already reached fedora and the image `quay.io/podman/stable`, so now real users start to be impacted by this issue.

Lgets get the quick fix in, and then continue to work on the long time fix.

LGTM

LGTM
and happy green test buttons

Thanks for merging this.

For the existing release 4.3, you will also cherry-pick this commit for any potential upcoming release 4.3.2 ?
